Madeira FC · 2025–26 Season

A home for girls who love the game — and want to grow with it.

We're a Girls U11 team at Madeira FC, led by head coach Mark Zara and assistant coach Jim Rodarte. Development-first. Positive coaching. Local competition. A tight-knit group that plays the game the right way.

Join Us at Tryouts → Meet the Coaches
About the Team

Part of Madeira FC. Built for this group of girls.

We play under the Madeira FC banner — a club rooted in community and a hometown feel with club-level competition. This page is about our specific team: the Girls U11 squad Mark and Jim coach together.

The group has been together two full seasons. Our girls are young 4th graders, and next year the leagues move from birth-year age divisions to grade-level play — which means they'll finally be matched with a true peer group instead of lining up against 5th graders. With two seasons of development already behind them, we think they're ready to move up at least two levels next season. Not because we chase records — because the girls have earned it.

Age Group
Girls U11
Practices
Twice a week
Seasons
Spring & Fall
Travel
Greater Cincinnati
Meet the Team Behind the Team

Coaches & Trainer

Two coaches who grew up with the game and a dedicated trainer working with the girls year-round. Positive, hands-on, and known to every player by name.

Head Coach
Mark Zara

Mark played soccer through high school and still plays in adult leagues for fun. He brings 10 years of coaching experience and is on the US Soccer Grassroots coaching pathway.

His focus is on finding individualized areas of improvement for each player — helping the girls make small, incremental gains every day — while passing on his love of the game.

Jim Rodarte
Assistant Coach

Jim played club soccer through high school and grew up with a real passion for the sport. His daughter Alex plays on the team.

What he loves most about coaching is watching the girls grow — their skill, their understanding of the game, and the life lessons the sport can teach. He's focused on making sure every player enjoys the game, keeps improving at her own pace, and builds lasting relationships with her teammates.

Teryn Otto
Technical Trainer

A former professional player with the North Carolina Courage and a decorated collegiate career at Asbury University, Teryn returned to Kentucky to train young soccer players in the Cincinnati and Northern Kentucky area.

Her focus is simple: give players the technical skills and confidence to elevate their game. Teryn runs Team Teryn Elite, serves as an assistant coach for the Kings Hammer USL-W team in Northern Kentucky, and has worked with players from programs like Notre Dame Academy, Lexington Christian Academy, and West Jessamine High School.

How We Coach

Our Philosophy

We believe in learning the game the right way — even when that means working through losses to get there. Our sidelines are positive, our practices are purposeful, and our focus is on the long game.

01

Development over wins

We measure success by how the girls grow — technically, tactically, and as people. Wins are a by-product of real development, not the goal.

02

Play the game the right way

We work the ball out of the back. We want to be the best passing team in our division. We teach the girls to think one play ahead — scan the field, have a plan, then make the pass.

03

Positive coaching, always

You won't catch us yelling negative things on the sideline. Instruction is positive. Reinforcement is specific. We're not harsh critics — we're teachers who believe confidence is a tool the girls need on the field.

04

1% better every day

A core principle from Coach Mark: the effort the girls put in at practice shapes how much they improve week over week. We want practice to be fun — and we want every girl pushing herself to be a little better than the day before.

Team Culture

A tight-knit group that lifts each other up.

  • Fun and focused.

    Practices are enjoyable — and they're also intentional. The girls come out ready to work, and they leave having gotten something out of it.

  • Supportive of each other.

    We build a culture where every girl has her teammates' backs — in wins, in losses, and especially when someone is having a hard game.

  • Two-sport athletes welcome.

    If your daughter plays another sport or has other commitments, we'll work with that. We believe kids are better athletes — and better people — when they get to pursue what they love.

  • Known by their coaches.

    Every girl is seen. We take time to understand what each player needs to grow, and we follow up with parents one-on-one about her development.

A moment that captures the team
“One of our players played goalie for the first time and struggled — she gave up a lot of goals and was devastated. Her teammates surrounded her after the game, encouraged her, lifted her back up. Her parents pulled me aside afterward just to say thank you. That moment said more about this team than any scoreline ever will.”
— Coach Jim
What to Expect

A Season, at a Glance

Consistent, manageable, and built around keeping the girls engaged without overloading them or their families.

🍂

Fall Season

Optional practices in July. Mandatory practices begin in August, 2× per week through late October or early November. One to two tournaments.

❄️

Off-Season & Indoor

Optional training in November–December. Optional indoor league January–February — nearly all girls participate.

🌱

Spring Season

Twice-weekly practices resume in March. Games run April through early June.

📍

Practice Schedule

Tuesdays 6:30–8:00 PM · Thursdays 4:30–5:30 PM
McDonald Commons, Madeira

How We Track Growth

Every contribution counts. We recognize all of it.

This season, we've implemented a custom app that tracks game-time minutes and stats across every aspect of the game — not just goals.

The intent isn't to judge the girls by their numbers. It's to give them insight into where they're improving and where they're contributing to the team.

After every match, we recognize players with pins — earned for any number of things, from great effort to using a skill we've been teaching in practice. It's how we celebrate growth that doesn't always show up on the scoreboard.

What we track

  • Goals
  • Assists
  • Great passes
  • Great effort
  • Skills from practice
  • Tackles
  • Interceptions
  • Clearances
  • Goalie saves
  • & more
Be Honest About the Fit

Is This Team Right for Your Daughter?

We want every family to feel at home here — and we'd rather be honest than oversell. Here's who thrives on our team.

You'll love it here if…

  • ✓Your daughter loves soccer and wants to get better.
  • ✓You value teamwork, community, and kids being known by their coaches.
  • ✓You want positive coaching — no sideline yelling or harsh criticism.
  • ✓You appreciate local competition and a manageable travel schedule.
  • ✓Your daughter plays other sports too, and you want coaches who respect that.

We're probably not for you if…

  • —You're looking for the most competitive league in the region.
  • —You want heavy travel — regional or national tournaments every weekend.
  • —You expect winning to come before development.
  • —You're hoping for a team where playing time is dictated by the scoreboard.
Looking Ahead

Plans for Next Season

Our goal for 2026–27 is to move up at least two levels from our current placement. The exact jump will depend on the players we add to the roster and where we are technically at the end of this season. We've seen rapid growth as a group over the last two seasons — and we expect that to continue.

Come out to tryouts.

The best way to know if this is the right fit is to meet the team, meet the coaches, and see the culture in person. We'd love to have your daughter join us.

Tryout Details
Mid-to-late May · During regular practice times
Tuesdays 6:30–8:00 PM  ·  Thursdays 4:30–5:30 PM
McDonald Commons, Madeira
Official club offers made in late May for the Fall 2026 season.
Annual fees: ~$1,200 — covers uniforms, league registration, field rental, coaching, referees, equipment, indoor league, and tournaments. No surprise add-ons.
